title: Inventory satellite LST, green-space, population vulnerability, and heat-mortality data sources for LMIC cities
outcome: A dated source inventory that separates satellite LST products (Landsat, MODIS), green-space mapping data (NDVI, urban tree canopy), population vulnerability data (WorldPop, informal-settlement maps), and heat-mortality data into usable, limited, and rejected categories with explicit LMIC city-coverage assessment.
inputs: Landsat thermal-band documentation and urban LST validation studies
MODIS LST product documentation for urban heat-island mapping
NDVI and urban tree-canopy mapping methodology
WorldPop gridded population and vulnerability data
Published informal-settlement mapping studies for target LMIC cities
expected_artifact: datasets.md update plus evidence.json records for each accepted source.
validation_method: Reviewer checks each listed source for URL, date, access status, license, spatial resolution, temporal coverage, and documented limitations — particularly LST-to-air-temperature validation in tropical urban contexts.
done_condition: At least five candidate data sources are classified as usable, limited, or rejected with explicit reasons covering resolution, LMIC city coverage, temperature-validation status, and green-space-type differentiation capability.

title: Map green-space distribution and cooling effects at neighborhood scale for at least two LMIC cities
outcome: A neighborhood-scale green-space and cooling-effect analysis for at least two LMIC cities, documenting green-space types (parks, street trees, green roofs), NDVI-derived vegetation density, LST-derived heat-island patterns, and explicit LST-to-air-temperature validation limitations.
inputs: Landsat or MODIS LST data for target cities
NDVI or urban tree-canopy data for green-space classification
Urban boundary and neighborhood-level administrative data
Ground weather-station data for LST-to-air-temperature validation where available
expected_artifact: validation.md update with green-space cooling methodology and city-level results.
validation_method: Replicator confirms LST data extraction, green-space classification methodology, and LST-to-air-temperature validation documentation for each target city.
done_condition: Green-space cooling analysis includes methodology, neighborhood-level results, green-space-type differentiation, LST-to-air-temperature limitations, and replication status for at least two cities.

title: Estimate neighborhood-level heat vulnerability by combining green-space cooling capacity, population exposure, and informal-settlement data
outcome: A vulnerability-weighted heat-risk analysis identifying neighborhoods with the largest gap between green-space cooling capacity and heat-exposure vulnerability, with explicit documentation of informal-settlement green-space deficits and irrigation-dependence constraints.
inputs: WorldPop gridded population and vulnerability data
expected_artifact: outputs.md update with vulnerability methodology and irrigation-dependence documentation.
validation_method: Replicator confirms vulnerability-weighting methodology, informal-settlement data integration, and irrigation-dependence documentation for each target city.
done_condition: Vulnerability analysis includes methodology, neighborhood-level results, informal-settlement deficit documentation, irrigation-dependence constraints, and replication status.

title: Red-team cooling-effect overstatement and green-gentrification risks
outcome: A failure-mode review identifying how cooling-effect estimates could overstate benefits by transferring temperate-city findings to tropical LMIC contexts, how green-space improvement could trigger gentrification that displaces vulnerable populations, and how irrigation constraints could make cooling predictions unrealistic.
expected_artifact: outputs.md and SAFETY.md linked review note.
validation_method: Reviewer confirms every cooling-effect claim has LST-to-air-temperature limitations, LMIC-transferability caveats, and irrigation-dependence documentation. Second reviewer replicates the failure-mode check.
done_condition: Every cooling-effect claim is either blocked, softened to LMIC-validation-limited language with explicit transferability caveats, or gated behind domain review and replicated red-team review.

title: Check whether outputs match municipal planning and informal-settlement upgrading decision needs
outcome: A field-facing review of whether the cooling-vulnerability analysis would change any real decision for municipal planning departments, urban forestry programs, or informal-settlement upgrading initiatives without creating perverse incentives.
expected_artifact: playbooks.md update with use and non-use guidance for municipal and informal-settlement audiences.
validation_method: Field reviewer identifies the actual decision, user, timing, and misuse risk — particularly the risk that green-space improvement triggers gentrification displacing the very populations it aims to protect, or that irrigation-dependent cooling predictions are unrealistic in water-scarce cities. Second reviewer replicates the non-use-case check.
done_condition: The output has a named user (e.g., Nairobi City County urban planning director), named decision (e.g., green-space investment targeting in informal settlements), explicit non-use cases (e.g., not for green-gentrification justification, not for irrigation-dependent cooling promises in water-scarce cities), and replicated field-reality review.
